## [00:00 - 03:02]

This is a pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer. Let's go! Alright, so first look. I completely understand. Sometimes Facebook rejects your ads. Sometimes they do it by mistake. I've had ads rejected too. Sometimes you accidentally misunderstand rules. When you get an ad rejected, that's absolutely happening to me. It actually just happened earlier this week when it's promoting the pubcast, has a beer on it, is going to people in the wrong countries, wrong ages, etc. And look, sometimes Facebook bans accounts for seemingly no reason. This message is not for you. Understand there are people who abuse Facebook's rules. Those people are the problem. Just make an attempt to do things the right way. Make an attempt to understand Facebook's rules. Make an attempt to behave ethically. It's not that hard. There are a ton of black hat practices out there right now. And I understand that they may provide some short-term gains. And it's hard to see that. And it may seem like everyone in your circle is doing it, whatever this stuff is. But you've got to vet the people you trust. Like, is there a reason you are listening to this person? Many of the same people telling you to do these shady things are most likely to get shut down. Maybe not today, maybe not tomorrow, but soon. They'll complain. Don't think that your complaints are equal to theirs. They deserved it. You are not the same. So play the long game. If you're making a constant attempt to do things the right way, you're just much less likely to get ads rejected and deal with that frustration. You're much less likely to get your ad account shut down and deal with all that madness. It doesn't guarantee these things won't happen, of course, but you're given a far better chance. That's life. It's also applicable to Facebook ads. Not everyone who does things the right way will benefit, but it gives you a far better chance to succeed long term than those fools who aim to bend and break the rules. This has been a pubcast shot.
